data.title: Bicara falls after early-stage trial data for head and neck cancer therapy
data.description:
Bicara Therapeutics (NASDAQ:BCAX) plunged ~43% in the morning hours on Friday after the Boston, Massachusetts-based biotech updated data from an early-stage trial designed to test its lead asset, ficerafusp alfa, as part of a combination regimen against a type of head and neck cancer.

Citing interim results from its Phase 1/1b trial, which tested the bifunctional antibody with pembrolizumab, marketed as Keytruda by Merck (MRK), the company said the drug combo has led to a 64% objective response rate as of Dec. 16.

The data cut also indicated that 21% of patients had achieved complete responses, and they indicated a median progression-free survival of 9.8 months and a 12-month overall survival rate of 61%.

While the study has yet to reach median overall survival and median DOR, the median overall survival rate has already exceeded 20 months.

Bicara (NASDAQ:BCAX) added that the drug combination generated a safety profile consistent with prior studies.  

The trial was designed to test ficerafusp alfa with pembrolizumab as a first-line therapy for recurrent/metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.

The company is set to present additional data, including results for overall survival and duration of response, on June 1 following a presentation at the upcoming annual meeting of the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO).

data.title: Intchains targets RMB 180M–200M in H1 2025 revenue with expanded altcoin mining lineup
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Intchains Group Limited (ICG) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Chaowei Yan, Chief Financial Officer, opened by noting, "We are pleased to report solid operational performance despite a challenging macroeconomic environment and a significant volatility in crypto market, particularly an 18% decline in the total market cap of cryptocurrency during the quarter." He highlighted that revenue reached $18.2 million, a 78.5% quarter-over-quarter increase, and surpassed the previously guided range of $15 million to $16 million for Q1 2025. Yan also reported, "Gross margin grew by 286.9% with gross margin improving to 56.9%, up from 26.2% last quarter." Operating income turned positive at $5.1 million, a recovery from the Q4 2024 loss.
  • The launch of the Aleo series miners was a key driver, with these products accounting for nearly 75% of total revenue. Yan stated, "Our AE MAX miner currently ranked fifth in daily profitability among all the mining machines, outperforming most of bitcoin miners." The Goldshell Byte, a new Doge mining machine with swappable mining cards, was launched in March, targeting flexibility for miners.
  • Yan emphasized Intchains' unique position: "Our differentiated strategy focused on blue ocean altcoin mining sector enables us to maintain gross margin higher than industry average for bitcoin miners, regardless of market cycles."
  • The company increased its Ether holdings by over 23% to 7,023 ETH, maintaining a long-term accumulation strategy.
  • Yan projected, "We plan to continue refining our Dogecoin miners through chip upgrades and enhanced product design, aiming to become the top 10 player in Dogecoin mining equipment."

Outlook

  • Yan provided revenue guidance for the first half of 2025: "We project the first quarter -- first half of 2025 revenue of approximately RMB 180 million to RMB 200 million."
  • Management indicated optimism for Q2 and beyond, citing improving market sentiment, anticipated interest rate cuts, and continued product innovation.
  • Guidance language was framed around market volatility and tariffs, with no explicit revision to longer-term projections compared to prior guidance.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 was $18.2 million, exceeding the high end of previous guidance and marking the highest quarterly revenue since 2023. The Aleo miner series contributed nearly 75% of the total.
  • Operating income was $5.1 million, a turnaround from an operating loss in Q4 2024, driven by top line growth and reduced R&D expenses.
  • Gross margin improved to 56.9%, up from 26.2% in the previous quarter, attributed to the high margins of Aleo miners and product optimization.
  • Net income was negative $4.7 million, primarily due to a $9.8 million noncash loss from revaluation of cryptocurrency holdings, mainly from a 46% drop in Ethereum price. Non-GAAP net income was negative $4.4 million.
  • As of March 31, 2025, cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaled $74.9 million, higher than year-end 2024.

Q&A

  • Mark Palmer, The Benchmark Company, asked how the revenue mix could shift given Aleo miners' dominance and the launch of new Doge miners. Yan responded that while Aleo miners would remain competitive, "our future revenue will come from Doge miners, Aleo miners and new miners maybe launched in second or third quarter."
  • Palmer inquired about Goldshell Byte's revenue potential and rollout timing. Yan explained the product addresses miners' pain points by allowing easy switching between cryptocurrencies, aiming to broaden the customer base rather than dominate revenue.
  • Minty Wang added that while Aleo series miners will likely drive first-half revenues, "some revenues may come from the Goldshell Byte product" in the second half, but exact percentages are uncertain and market-dependent.
  • Matthew Galinko, Maxim Group, questioned inventory levels and outlook. Yan replied, "most of our inventory are inventory for Dogecoin miners, and some of them are Aleo miners related chip inventory," with plans to sell existing inventory depending on market conditions.
  • Galinko followed up on Goldshell Byte's appeal to consumers. Yan stated the product was well received by new customers and serves as a strategy to expand the customer base, while Minty Wang estimated a payback period of "within 400 days" for users.
  • Galinko also asked about R&D spending trends. Yan indicated uncertainty, citing ongoing projects but noted, "now we cannot actually predict when to do the wafer tape-outs at this time."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment, congratulating the company for strong operating performance amid industry challenges and inquiring about future product mix and market strategy.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, frequently referencing product success and strategic positioning, while acknowledging headwinds such as crypto price declines and market volatility. In Q&A, Yan demonstrated openness but was cautious about providing precise forecasts, especially regarding revenue breakdown and R&D timing.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from cautious optimism to greater confidence, supported by strong revenue growth and margin improvement. Analyst tone moved from general optimism about crypto market rebounds to more targeted questioning about product mix and inventory management.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for the first half of 2025 shifted from RMB 200 million–250 million in Q4 2024 to RMB 180 million–200 million, reflecting a more conservative near-term outlook.
  • Product focus evolved: Q4 2024 anticipated Aleo and Dogecoin miners as key drivers, while Q1 2025 highlighted Aleo series as the primary contributor and introduced Goldshell Byte as a potential growth lever.
  • Gross margin rebounded sharply in Q1 2025 after a dip in Q4 2024, driven by the success of Aleo miners versus previous lower-margin Dogecoin machines.
  • Management's confidence increased as operational results improved, while analysts shifted from broad market questions to more detailed inquiries regarding product specifics and strategic execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited significant volatility in the crypto market, including an 18% decline in total market cap and sharp price drops for Ether, Dogecoin, and Aleo.
  • Yan mentioned potential impacts from tariffs and ongoing market volatility affecting revenue projections.
  • Inventory risk was noted, with Yan stating, "we will try our best to sell these inventories to the market instead of to write-off the inventory."
  • Noncash revaluation losses on Ethereum holdings materially impacted net income, underscoring exposure to crypto asset price swings.

Final Takeaway

Intchains Group delivered its highest quarterly revenue since 2023, propelled by the successful launch of Aleo miners and improved gross margins, while navigating significant market volatility and repositioning its revenue outlook for the first half of 2025. The company remains committed to product innovation, Ether accumulation, and broadening its customer base through new launches like Goldshell Byte, positioning itself for growth as market sentiment and industry conditions evolve.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Booz Allen plunges on quarterly revenue miss, weak outlook
data.description:
Booz Allen Hamilton (NYSE:BAH) -13.7% in early trading Friday after reporting FQ4 adjusted earnings that matched analyst estimates but revenues that came up short, and issuing below-consensus FY 2026 guidance alongside layoffs due to federal cost-cutting efforts.

Booz Allen (NYSE:BAH) generates nearly all its annual revenue from contracts in which the end client is a U.S. government agency or department.

Q4 net profit rose to $193M, or $1.52/share, from $128M, or $0.98/share, in the year-earlier quarter, while revenue rose 7.3% Y/Y to $2.97B but missed the $3.03B analyst consensus estimate.

The company forecast FY 2026 adjusted earnings of $6.20-$6.55/share on revenues of $12B-$12.5B, below analyst expectations of $6.88/share in adjusted earnings and $12.82B in revenues, as well as adjusted EBITDA of $1.32B-$1.37B.

CEO Horacio Rozanski said on the earnings conference call that the Trump administration's focus on cutting costs is affecting the company's contracts with civilian agencies, leading the company to "restructure and reset" its civil business, with cost reductions and layoffs "to match anticipated demand," according to Investopedia.

Booz Allen (BAH) expects to reduce its workforce by ~7% in its Q1, "heavily concentrated" in its civil business, CFO Matt Calderone said on the call, while it sees its defense and intelligence segments to continue growing this year; as of March 31, the company had 35,800 employees, meaning layoffs likely would affect ~2,500 staff.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: Nuclear stocks, WDAY, INTU, and more
data.description:
Stock futures slipped Friday after President Donald Trump warned that iPhones not made in the U.S. could face tariffs of 25% or more, and threatened a 50% levy on European Union goods starting June 1, 2025.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) +9% — Shares surged after the maker of QuickBooks, TurboTax, and Credit Karma posted a Q3 double beat and raised its FQ4 and full-year outlook. For Q4, the company expects adjusted EPS of $2.63–$2.68 (midpoint $2.66 vs. $2.60 consensus) and revenue of $3.723B–$3.760B (midpoint $3.74B vs. $3.53B consensus), reflecting 17%–18% Y/Y growth. Intuit also raised its full-year revenue guidance to $18.723B (~15% Y/Y growth), up from the prior 12%–13%, citing strong performance in Credit Karma and its Consumer Group.
  • Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) +3% — Shares rose after the company beat Q1 FY26 estimates with 15% Y/Y revenue growth and also provided an upbeat outlook. For Q2, Autodesk guided revenue between $1.72B and $1.73B, surpassing the consensus of $1.7B, and adjusted EPS of $2.44–$2.48, much above the $2.34 estimate. For the full year, Autodesk sees revenue of $6.92B–$6.99B (midpoint $6.955B vs. $6.93B consensus).
  • Uranium Energy (NYSE:UEC) +19% – Shares of nuclear energy companies surged amid reports that President Donald Trump could issue executive orders later today aimed at revitalizing the U.S. nuclear industry. Reuters cited sources saying the orders may streamline reactor approvals and boost domestic fuel supply chains. Power demand in the U.S. is projected to reach record levels in 2025 and 2026, fueled by energy-intensive AI and crypto data centers. Uranium miners like Energy Fuels (NYSE:UUUU) +13% and Centrus Energy (NYSE:LEU) +20%, while Cameco (NYSE:CCJ) +7% and the Global X Uranium ETF (URA) rose nearly 10%. Developers of new nuclear tech also jumped, with Sam Altman-backed Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) soaring nearly 20%, NANO Nuclear Energy (NASDAQ:NNE) up 19%, and NuScale Power rising over 15%.

Biggest stock losers

  • Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) -6% — Shares slid despite Q1 results and guidance that were mostly in line. The company reiterated Q2 subscription revenue guidance at $2.16B (matching estimates) and raised its full-year adjusted operating margin forecast to 28.5% (up from 28%). Full-year subscription revenue is expected to reach $8.8B.
  • Deckers Outdoor (NYSE:DECK) -16% – Shares plunged despite solid Q4 results, as the company issued a weak FQ1 forecast and withheld full-year guidance amid macro uncertainty. For FQ1, sales are projected between $890M and $910M, below the $921.65M consensus, while EPS is expected in the range of $0.62 to $0.67, falling short of the $0.79 estimate. On the capital return front, the board approved a $2.25B boost to its stock repurchase authorization, bringing the total to approximately $2.5B.
  • Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST) -11% – Shares slumped after the company reported a Q1 top-line beat but warned that new tariffs could cut Q2 EPS by $0.11 to $0.16, prompting investor concern. CEO Jim Conroy noted ongoing macro and geopolitical uncertainty, including prolonged inflation and evolving trade policies, with over half of Ross's merchandise originating from China. The retailer pulled its full-year guidance and now expects Q2 same-store sales to be flat to +3%, with EPS projected between $1.40 and $1.55, well below the $1.66 consensus and down from $1.59 last year.
  • Booz Allen (NYSE:BAH) -17% – Shares slumped after the company reported mixed FQ4 results, with non-GAAP EPS of $1.61 matching estimates, but revenue of $2.97B (+7.2% Y/Y) missing by $60M. Despite posting a record Q4 backlog of $37B and a solid trailing 12-month book-to-bill ratio of 1.39x, the quarterly book-to-bill fell to 0.71x. FY25 guidance further weighed on sentiment, with projected revenue of $12B–$12.5B (midpoint $12.25B) below the $12.83B consensus and adjusted EPS of $6.20–$6.55 (midpoint $6.38), well short of expectations for $6.88.

data.title: Gladstone Investment invests in Florida-based Sun State Nursery & Landscaping
data.description:
Gladstone Investment (NASDAQ:GAIN) is making an investment in un State Nursery & Landscaping, LLC, in partnership with Everglades Equity, LLC, it said on Friday.

Based in Jacksonville, Florida, Sun State is a commercial landscaping installation and maintenance provider. 

"We are looking forward to working with Everglades, President Paul Kassab, and the team at Sun State to build on the company's established reputation in the Jacksonville market," said Christopher Lee, senior managing director of Gladstone Investment.

Terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

GAIN shares edged up 0.7% in Friday morning trading.

data.title: SpringWorks gets EU endorsement for genetic disorder treatment
data.description:
SpringWorks Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SWTX) said that an advisory panel of the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed Mirdametinib, as a treatment for patients with neurofibromatosis type 1, aged 2 years and older.

Issuing a so-called positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the marketing authorization for the company's MEK inhibitor, Mirdametinib as a treatment for symptomatic, inoperable plexiform neurofibromas. This includes pediatric and adult patients with neurofibromatosis type 1, a genetic disorder.

The CHMP recommendation will next be reviewed by the European Commission (EC) before a final decision on marketing authorization, which is expected in the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Wolfspeed appoints former executive vice president to newly created COO position
data.description:
Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) has appointed David Emerson, an industry veteran and former executive vice president with the company, to the newly created role of chief operating officer to help the silicon carbide technology company improve gross margins and return to profitability.

Shares climbed 5% during early market action on Friday.

On Tuesday, it was reported Wolfspeed was planning to file bankruptcy at some point in the next few weeks. As a result, shares plunged 59% by the close of Wednesday's trading session. They rebounded 34% on Thursday. Still, shares are down 74% year to date, and are trading at less than $2 per share, or about 1.4% of the all-time high of $137 set in November 2021.

Emerson, who formerly served as the executive vice president of Wolfspeed's LED Products division when the company was still called Cree, was hired to run the Operations, Supple Chain and Quality divisions.

"Having previously led our LED business through market disruption and global expansion, Dave brings a wealth of industry expertise and strategic insight which positions him well to drive operational excellence at Wolfspeed," said Wolfspeed CEO Robert Fuerle. 

Emerson is expected to play a critical role in improving manufacturing quality and efficiency, accelerating time-to-market, and ensuring consistent delivery.

"My experience driving operational excellence has equipped me with the skills to help develop best-in-class solutions for our blue-chip customers, all while working to accelerate the Company's path to profitability," Emerson said.

Seeking Alpha analysts point to Wolfspeed's shrinking gross margins as evidence of the company's severe struggles.

"Wolfspeed posted Q3 earnings on May 8, and it became even clearer to me that this company has no future," said Seeking Alpha analyst Josh Arnold. "Revenue fell from $201M to $185M year-over-year, despite Mohawk Valley Fab contributing $78M, up from $28M a year ago."

"The larger issue, in my view, is the fact that Wolfspeed's gross margins continued their freefall," he added. "Q3 of last year saw adjusted gross margin of 15% of revenue, but that fell to just 2% in this year's Q3. I don't recall any company I've ever seen post gross margins near zero. No business can survive that way."

Seeking Alpha analyst Henrik Alex said if Wolfspeed ends up filing a Chapter 11 bankruptcy, it's likely common equity holders would end up with nothing.

"With $6.65B in debt ranking ahead of the common equity, a recovery for shareholders in bankruptcy looks highly unlikely," Alex said. "For my part, I would expect unsecured creditors to emerge as the restructured company's new owners, with current equity holders likely being wiped out."

data.title: Aequus Pharmaceuticals announces sale of Zimed PF to Luvo Medical Technologies
data.description:
• Aequus Pharmaceuticals (OTCQB:AQSZF) and Luvo Medical Technologies announced on Friday the signing of an asset purchase agreement for the sale of Zimed PF.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Luvo will pay Aequus a lump sum payment of C$400,000 at the closing of the transaction and an ongoing royalty of 6% on gross profit attributable to the sale of Zimed PF until July 29th, 2029.
  • Until closing, Luvo will be responsible for managing distribution and commercialization efforts of Zimed PF. If existing inventory is not depleted by closing, Luvo will purchase the outstanding inventory.
  • The Transaction is expected to close in Q4 2025 upon the transfer of the Drug Identification Number.
  • Aequus expects to call a special meeting of shareholders to be held in Q3, 2025 for approval of the Transaction.
  • Source: Press Release
